member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.DataSet)
member.summary: Send a DataSet object onto a remote client without serializing relationships.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.DataSet,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Send a DataSet object onto a remote client. The parameter bNeedRelations is defaulted to false. If bNeedRelations is set to true, relationships among tables are remoted.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.DataSet,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Send a DataSet object onto a remote client. Parameters bNeedRelations and nBatchSize are defaulted to false and 10240, repectively. If bNeedRelations is set to true, relationships among tables are remoted. The parameter nBatchSize controls network communication batch size in byte. By default, nBatchSize is 10240.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.DataTable,System.Int32)
member.summary: Send a DataTable object onto a remote client. Parameter nBatchSize is defaulted to 10240, which controls network communication batch size in byte.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.DataTable)
member.summary: Send a DataTable object onto a remote client.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.IDataReader,System.Int32)
member.summary: Send a set of records through the interface IDataReader onto a remote client. Parameter nBatchSize is defaulted to 10240. The parameter nBatchSize controls network communication batch size in byte. If proper, this method is preferred for reduction of memory footprint and latency without pre-loading data records into memory.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer.Send(System.Data.IDataReader)
member.summary: Send a set of records through the interface IDataReader onto a remote client. If proper, this method is preferred for reduction of memory footprint and latency without pre-loading data records into memory.

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CAdoClientPeer
member.summary: An class makes remoting ADO.NET objects, DataSet, DataTable and IDataReader onto a remote client much easier. To use this class at server side, your client peer class must derive from CAdoClientPeer instead of CClientPeer.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.RemoveFileExtension(System.String)
member.summary: remove a file extension.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.AddFileExtension(System.String)
member.summary: add a file extension.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.FileExtensions
member.summary: An array of file extensions supported for downloading by default. To add or remove file extensions, use the methods AddFileExtension and RemoveFileExtension, respectively.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.BrowserIpAddress
member.summary: Browser IP address which a unique chat id is associated with. SocketPro HTTP push service will associate a chat id with an IP address when a browser calls the JavaScript method enter. If the IP address is not recognized, the push service will return false for all of the other calls. This design makes security better especially for cross-site HTTP push.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.ListeningTimeout
member.summary: Listening timeout in ms. It defaults to 60,000 (60 seconds) for most of major browsers. Don't change the default value unless you must do so.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.LeaseTime
member.summary: Lease time in ms. It defaults to 120,000 (120 seconds) for most of major browsers. Don't change the default value unless you must do so.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.CrossSite
member.summary: The property indicates if the current request is a cross-site HTTP request originating (ujsonxml.js) from SocketProAdapter for JavaScript. You may override this property for your requirements.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.Default
member.summary: A default file name with relative path.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.PushPath
member.summary: A case-sensitive string indicating a chat path or channel. It defaults to '/UCHAT'.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.OnDownloading(System.String)
member.summary: The virtual function is called right before downloading a file (strFile). If the virtual function returns false, there is no downloading. You can allow or dis-allow file downloading by overriding this virtual function.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.OnProcessingRequest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: The virtual function is called where there is a HTTP request coming from a web browser through SocketProAdapter for JavaScript. The return value will be sent to a browser.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.OnAfterProcessingHttpPushRequest(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: The virtual function is called right after processing HTTP push request.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.OnBeforeProcessingHttpPushRequest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: The virtual function is called right before processing HTTP push request.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.OnProcessingHttpPushRequestException(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: The virtual function is called if there is an exception (myError) during processing.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.IsOk(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: A virtual function which can be used for blocking chatting HTTP requests according to ip address. If the virtual function returns false, there is no joining for HTTP server push. For example, this is especially useful to cross-site HTTP chat requests for better security.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer.DoCallback(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Send a message (strMsg) to a browser through this socket connection from a given chat id (strChatId). The method returns true if successful.

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPushPeer
member.summary: A base class for implementing HTTP server push and supporting HTTP request from a web browser through SocketProAdapter for JavaScript. The class represents a simplified HTTP service supporting two HTTP commands, GET and POST. It also supports downloading files which belong to one of pre-defined file extensions (see the property FileExtensions).

member.summary: A property for MS IE8 browser or later

member.summary: A url address with case-sensitive chatting path or channel like http://www.somedomain.com:20901/UCHAT

member.summary: A relative path to the file ujsonxml.js. It defaults to an empty string (web site root).

member.summary: A version string like 1.1 by default.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.IComet.Enter(System.Web.UI.Page,System.String,System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong[],System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Join one or more chatting groups (Groups) with a given case-insensitive user id. The parameters page and strOnMessage indicate asp.net page and JavaScript function, respectively. A new JavaScript code snippet is created, and already registerred at startup block containing UHTTP default configuration code within asp.net page. On a browser side, it will kick off listening messages automatically in a given delay time (nDelayTime). The method returns a string containing a chat id if successful. Note that the method is thread-safe.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.IComet.Enter(System.Web.UI.Page,System.String,System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong[],System.String)
member.summary: Join one or more chatting groups (Groups) with a given case-insensitive user id. The parameters page and strOnMessage indicate asp.net page and JavaScript function, respectively. A new JavaScript code snippet is created, and already registerred at startup block containing UHTTP default configuration code within asp.net page. On a browser side, it will kick off listening messages automatically after 350 ms. The method returns a string containing a chat id if successful. Note that the method is thread-safe.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.IComet.Enter(System.String,System.String,System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong[])
member.summary: Join one or more chatting groups (Groups) with a given case-insensitive user id. The parameter strSubscriberIpAddress indicates a subscriber ip address like '111.222.121.212'. The method returns a string containing a chat id if successful. Note that the method is thread-safe.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite.OpenRowset(System.String,System.String,UDBLib.tagCursorType,System.Int32,System.Int16,System.Int32)
member.summary: Execute a SQL statement for a rowset. If the rowset is opened with the hint AsynFetch, a rowset will be asynchronously fetched at the fastest speed and be closed afterwards. Otherwise, the rowset will be fetched with the first batch of records.
member.param: strSQL is a SQL query statement.
strTableName is a table name.
ct is a type of cursor (ctForwardOnly, ctStatic, ctKeyset, ctDynamic).
nHints represents a set of rowset properties like Readonly, Scrollable, AsynFetch, ......, and so on.
sBatchSize is the number of records per batch. If it is 0, remote database service will automatically determine the size.
nRowsExpected is a given number of records to be fetched. -1 means fetching all of records.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite.OpenRowset(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Execute a SQL statement for a readonly rowset. Fetch all of records at the fastest speed and close rowset afterwards

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite.ExecuteNonQuery(System.String)
member.summary: Execute a SQL statement without opening a rowset

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite.OpenRowsetFromParameters(System.Object[],System.String,UDBLib.tagCursorType,System.Int32,System.Int16,System.Int32)
member.summary: Execute a SQL statement for a rowset. If the rowset is opened with the hint AsynFetch, a rowset will be asynchronously fetched at the fastest speed and be closed afterwards. Otherwise, the rowset will be fetched with the first batch of records.
member.param: aParameterData is one set of parameter data.
strTableName is a table name.
ct is a type of cursor (ctForwardOnly, ctStatic, ctKeyset, ctDynamic).
nHints represents a set of rowset properties like Readonly, Scrollable, AsynFetch, ......, and so on.
sBatchSize is the number of records per batch. If it is 0, remote database service will automatically determine the size.
nRowsExpected is a given number of records to be fetched. -1 means fetching all of records.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite.OpenRowsetFromParameters(System.Object[],System.String)
member.summary: Open a rowset from one array (one set) of parameter data

member.summary: Open a command with a parameterized statement using an array of parameter infos. Later, you can use the method DoBatch to execute the parameterized statement in batch

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite.DoBatch(System.Object[])
member.summary: Send a matrix (multiple sets) of parameter data onto a backend database through a parameterized statement. You can use the method for fast add, update and delete as well as executing store procedures.

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CAsynDBLite
member.summary: This class is a wrapper of key features in the file udb.dll, which simplifies accessing a remote backend database from win32 or MS device platforms. With the help of the class, you can use one piece of code to access all of data sources through MS OLEDB or MS ODBC technology.

member.name: F:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.RemoteDB.CParamInfo.m_nLen
member.summary: Parameter max data length in byte. It is used only for data types sdVT_STR, sdVT_BYTES, and sdVT_WSTR. -1 means that the parameter would be a large BLOB or text. For other data types, it is ignored.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CBaseService.m_bUsePool
member.summary: if m_bUsePool is true, all of CClientPeer derived objects are put into m_Pool, and then deleted in a specified time (60 seconds) after a socket connection is closed. When the pool is enabled, it will be helpful for performance in case socket connections are repeatedly closed and connected. However, you may have to initialize a CClientPeer derived object by overriding CClientPeer::OnSwitchFrom because a CClientPeer derived object may be in a different state. If m_bUsePool is false, all of CClientPeer derived objects are deleted immediately after a socket connection is closed or swicthed.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CHttpPeerBase.CHttpPushImpl.Enter(System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong[],System.String,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Join one or more chat groups (GroupIds) through HTTP service with a given lease time. GroupIds can be null or nothing. If so, you can use the chat id string to send a message to other clients. If successful, the method returns a unique HTTP server chat session ID, which will be used by other HTTP chat methods. The returned chat session is associated with the ip address (strIpAddr) like '111.222.212.121'. If the input strIpAddr is null, empty or invalid, SocketPro will use the current client IP address instead.

The returned chat session is associated with this client IP address.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CClientPeer.AutoBuffer

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CSocketProServer.UseSSL(System.String,System.String,System.String,USOCKETLib.tagEncryptionMethod)
member.summary: Start a secured SocketPro server with either OpenSSL or MS SSPI with certificate and key file (strPfxFile) protected by a password (strPassword). Call this method before calling the method StartSocketProServer. If the parameter EncryptionMethod is either MSSSL or MSTLSv1, MS SSPI is used. Also, a certificate subject (strSubject) must be specified. If the parameter EncryptionMethod is either SSL23 or TLSv1, OpenSSL is used. No certificate subject (strSubject) is required.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CSocketProServer.UseSSL(USOCKETLib.tagEncryptionMethod,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Start a secured SocketPro server with MSSSL or MSTLSv1 using MS SSPI Call this method before calling the method StartSocketProServer. The parameter EncryptionMethod must be either MSSSL or MSTLSv1. The parameters strSubject, bMachine and bRoot indicate the certificate (strSubject) in a store (bMachine and bRoot).

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CSocketProServer.UseSSL(USOCKETLib.tagEncryptionMethod,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Start a secured SocketPro server with SSL23 or TLSv1 using OpenSSL Call this method before calling the method StartSocketProServer. The parameter EncryptionMethod must be either SSL23 or TLSv1.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ServerSide.CSocketProServer.StopSocketProServer

member.param: Events could be eOnSwitchTo, eOnIsPermitted, eOnClose, eOnAccept, eOnSSLEvent .....

member.summary: Shut down socket pool, and clean all of associated resources.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.Stop(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Stop parallel computation. If the parameter bCancelRequestsInQueue is set to true, socket pool will send a request Cancel to server for shutting down computation as soon as possible. If the parameter bCancelRequestsInQueue is set to false by default, it stops computation gracefully until all of scheduled jobs are finished.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.Stop
member.summary: Stop parallel computation gracefully until all of scheduled jobs are finished.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.Resume
member.summary: Call the method to resume parallel computation after calling the method Pause.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.Pause
member.summary: Pause parallel computation gracefully. After pausing, you can call the method Resume to resume parallel computation.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.Process
member.summary: Starts to process requests in parallel. If socket pool is not started or there is no job available, the method returns false.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.MakeConnection(SocketProAdapter.CConnectionContext)
member.summary: Make one connection to a remote SocketPro server specified by the parameter cc, and join parallel computation immediately if there is a job available. Note if there is no unconnected USocket object available, the method will return false. If the method returns false, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.WaitUntilFinished(System.Int32)
member.summary: Call this method to wait until a given timeout (nTimeout). If all jobs are indeed processed within the given time (nTimeout), the method returns true. Otherwise, it returns false. Don't call this method from the virtual functions, OnFailover, OnExecuteJob, OnResultReturned and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, there maybe is a dead lock if nTimeout is large.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.WaitUntilFinished
member.summary: Call this method to wait until all of jobs and tasks are processed with infinite time. If all jobs are indeed processed, the method returns true. Otherwise, it returns false. Don't call this method from the virtual functions, OnFailover, OnExecuteJob, OnResultReturned and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, there is a dead lock.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.AllLoaded
member.summary: A property indicating if all of connected sockets in pool are processing requests in parallel.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPoolEx`1.GetHandlersInParallel
member.summary: The method returns an array of handlers involved for proessing requests in parallel.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.IsStarted
member.summary: Check the pool is started.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.ShutdownPool
member.summary: Shut down socket pool and clean all of associated resources.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.MakeConnection(SocketProAdapter.CConnectionContext)
member.summary: Make one connection to a remote SocketPro server specified by the parameter cc. Note if there is no unconnected USocket object available, the method will return false. If the method returns false, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.StartSocketPool(System.String,System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong,System.String,System.String,System.Byte,System.Byte,USOCKETLib.tagEncryptionMethod,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Start a socket pool. All of sockets will be connected to one remote SocketPro server (strHost, lPort) with given credentials (strUID, strPassword). The bSocketsPerThread indicates the number of sockets hosted by a thread. The parameter bThreads determines the number of threads in pool. Specifically if bThread is zero, the number of threads in socket pool will equal to the number of processors of a machine. If it returns false, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.StartSocketPool(SocketProAdapter.CConnectionContext[],System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Start a socket pool. The array of connection contexts (ConnectionContexts) defines what and how these remote server are connected. The parameter bSocketsPerThread indicates the max number of sockets can be hosted per thread. It should not exceed 63. The parameter bThreads indicates the number of threads within the pool. As usual, it should be equal to the number of processors by setting the parameter to zero. If failed, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.StartSocketPool(SocketProAdapter.CConnectionContext[],System.Byte)
member.summary: Start a socket pool with the number of threads equal to the number of processors. The array of connection contexts (ConnectionContexts) defines what and how these remote server are connected. The parameter bSocketsPerThread indicates the max number of sockets can be hosted per thread. It should not exceed 63.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.StartSocketPool(System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Start a socket pool without connecting to any remote host. If the input bThread is zero, socket pool will use the number of processors instead. The input bSocketPerThread should be between 1 and 63. The pool will contain bSocketPerThread*bThreads sockets totally. If failed, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.StartSocketPool(System.Byte)
member.summary: Start a socket pool without connecting to any remote host. The input bSocketPerThread should be between 1 and 63. The pool will contain bSocketPerThread*(the number of processors) sockets totally.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CSocketPool`1.StartSocketPool(System.String,System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong,System.String,System.String,System.Byte)
member.summary: Start a socket pool. All of sockets will be connected to a remote SocketPro server (strHost, lPort) with given credentials (strUID, strPassword). The bSocketsPerThread indicates the number of sockets hosted by a thread. The number of threads in socket pool will equal to the number of processors of a machine. If it returns false, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.summary: Lock a socket with a given timeout (nTimeout) in ms. If pIUSocketSameThread is zero, pool will lock anyone of sockets available. If pIUSocketSameThread is pointed to an locked socket, pool will lock a socket that hosted with the same thread for the locked thread pointed by pIUSocketSameThread. If the function returns nothing, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.summary: Lock a socket with a given timeout (nTimeout) in ms. If the function returns nothing, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.summary: Lock a socket with infinite timeout. If the function returns nothing, refer to m_hr for error code.

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CClientSocket
member.summary: The class CClientSocket wraps all of commonly used functions found in the interfaces ISocketBase, IUSocket and IUFast. It also wraps the functions that can't be easily used through dotNet COM interop service. In case you need the functions that are not wrapped, you could use the method GetUSocket to get them through dotNet COM interop service. Additionally, each of instances of the class CClientSocket contains an instance of class CUQueue, which is used to help sending all of requests and retrieving their returned results automatically through the interface IUFast. The class CClientSocket dispatches all of returned results onto one of attched instances of CAsyncServiceHandler-derived classes on the fly. To monitor various socket events, you could use delegates m_OnXXXX as shown in samples and tutorials.

member.summary: A property for job recycle bin size. Larger job recycle bin will make calling the methods Wait stable without any error at the cost of some memory. The property defaults to 10 but will never be larger than 10240.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.ResetPosition(System.Int64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reset a queued job (jobId) to a new position (nNewPosition). If failed, the method returns -1. Otherwise, it returns a new position.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.UnlockIdentity(SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CAsyncServiceHandler)
member.summary: Unlock an identity.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.LockIdentity(System.Int32)
member.summary: Lock an identity with a given timeout from available async handlers in socket pool. You must call the method UnlockIdentity with the returned async handler later. Otherwise, async handler identities will be leaked quickly. The method will return null or nothing if no async handler is available at the calling time. Don't call this method from the virtual functions OnFailover, OnJobDone, OnExecutingJob, OnJobProcessing, OnReturnedResultProcessed and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, there maybe is a dead lock if nTimeout is large.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.LockIdentity
member.summary: Lock an identity infinitely from available async handlers in socket pool. You must call the method UnlockIdentity with the returned async handler later. Otherwise, async handler identities will be leaked quickly. The method will return null or nothing if no async handler is available at the calling time. Don't call this method from the virtual functions OnFailover, OnJobDone, OnExecutingJob, OnJobProcessing, OnReturnedResultProcessed and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, there maybe is a dead lock.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.DestroyJob(SocketProAdapter.IJobContext)
member.summary: Destroy a job (JobContext). If successful, it returns true. You may call this method after calling the method CreateJob.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.EnqueueJob(SocketProAdapter.IJobContext)
member.summary: Enqueue a job (JobContext). If successful, it returns true. Typically calling this method after calling the method CreateJob.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.CreateJob(System.Object,System.AsyncCallback)
member.summary: Create a job and associate it with an identity object (Identity) with a given callback (cb). If successful, it returns an interface to a job. After getting an interface to a job, you must call the method either EnqueueJob or DestroyJob. Without doing so, it may lead to memory leak. Note that SocketPro loading balance round robin computation is dependent on the given identity.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.CreateJob(System.Object)
member.summary: Create a job and associate it with an identity object (Identity) without setting a callback. If successful, it returns an interface to a job. After getting an interface to a job, you must call the method either EnqueueJob or DestroyJob. Without doing so, it may lead to memory leak or undesired effect. Note that SocketPro loading balance round robin computation is dependent on the given identity.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager.CreateJob
member.summary: Create a job without associating it with an identity object or setting a callback. If successful, it returns an interface to a job. After getting an interface to a job, you must call its method either EnqueueJob or DestroyJob. Without doing so, it may lead to memory leak or undesired effect.

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.IJobManager
member.summary: An interface for managing a set of jobs. It is important that you should not call any one of IJobManager::Wait(Any or All) methods from the functions OnFailover, OnJobDone, OnExecutingJob, OnJobProcessing, OnReturnedResultProcessed and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, maybe there is a dead lock if timeout is large. Note that all of methods and properties are thread-safe.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Handler
member.summary: A property for an associated aysnc handler. Note that the property will be null or nothing if the job status is not in running.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Callback
member.summary: A property indicating a callback. If it is not null or nothing, there will be a callback from a job when completed.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.BufferSize
member.summary: A property indicating the size of buffer used by this job in byte.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Size
member.summary: A property indicating the size of tasks in byte.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Bundled
member.summary: A property indicating if all of tasks are bundled. If all of tasks are bundled, all of tasks are passed from one loading balancer to another one. As usual, you should not set it to true. The property defaults to false.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Tasks
member.summary: A collection of the pairs of task id and its request context.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Identity
member.summary: An identity object associated with this job. Note that SocketPro loading balance round robin computation is dependent on this property.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.JobStatus
member.summary: The job status. Note that the property may indicate the order of queued jobs to be processed.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.CountOfTasks
member.summary: The number of tasks in a job.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.JobId
member.summary: A property indicating job id.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.JobManager
member.summary: A property indicating a reference to a job manager which creates this job.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.AllocateLargeMemory(System.Int32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong)
member.summary: Allocate a large memory chunk for this job. If successful, it returns true. If nSize is less than zero or very large, the method may be fail or cause other unknown problems. You must be very careful to allocate a large memory chunk.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Wait(System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait until this job is finished or canceled or timed out within a given timeout (nTimeout). This method is preferred over the property AsyncWaitHandle. The method returns false only if the job is timed out. If nTimeout equals to -1, the method waits infinitely until the job is either finished or canceled. Don't call this method from the virtual functions OnFailover, OnJobDone, OnExecutingJob, OnJobProcessing, OnReturnedResultProcessed and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, there maybe is a dead lock if nTimeout is large.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.Wait
member.summary: Wait infinitely until this job is finished or canceled. This method is preferred over the property AsyncWaitHandle. Don't call this method from the virtual functions OnFailover, OnJobDone, OnExecutingJob, OnJobProcessing, OnReturnedResultProcessed and OnSocketPoolEvent. Otherwise, there maybe is a dead lock.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.RemoveTask(System.Int32)
member.summary: Remove a task specified by idTask. It returns true if successful.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext.AddTask(System.Int16,System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.summary: Add a task into the job. It returns a non-zero task id if successful. A task consists of a request id (sRequestId), a buffer (pBuffer), and its size (nSize).

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.IJobContext
member.summary: An interface for managing jobs, and each of them may contain many tasks. Note that all of methods and properties are thread-safe.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IUPush.Exit
member.summary: Leave chat groups. The method returns true if successful.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IUPush.SendUserMessage(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Send an array of bytes (Message) onto one client identified with user id (UserId). The method returns true if successful.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IUPush.SendUserMessage(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Send a message (Message) onto one client identified with user id (UserId). The method returns true if successful.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IUPush.Broadcast(System.Byte[],System.Int32[])
member.summary: Send an array of bytes (Message) onto an array of chat groups (Groups) of clients. The method returns true if successful.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IUPush.Broadcast(System.Object,System.Int32[])
member.summary: Send a message (Message) onto an array of chat groups (Groups) of clients. The method returns true if successful.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.IUPush.Enter(System.Int32[])
member.summary: Enter an array of chat groups (Groups). The method returns true if successful.

member.name: T:SocketProAdapter.IUPush
member.summary: An interface for sending chat messages. Note that all of methods are thread-safe.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.CTaskContext.Client
member.summary: A boolean indicating if the request originally comes from a remote client.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.CTaskContext.Size
member.summary: A request input parameters size in byte.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.CTaskContext.RequestId
member.summary: A request identification number.

member.name: F:SocketProAdapter.tagJobStatus.jsRunning
member.summary: Job under processing.

member.name: F:SocketProAdapter.tagJobStatus.jsQueued
member.summary: Job queued but not in processing.

member.name: F:SocketProAdapter.tagJobStatus.jsCreating
member.summary: Job in creating state.

member.name: F:SocketProAdapter.tagJobStatus.jsInitial
member.summary: Job in recycle pool for reuse.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CAsyncServiceHandler.Wait
member.summary: Wait until the last request is processed and its result is returned. The method returns true if its result is returned. Otherwise, it returns false. Refer to the method CClientSocket.Wait.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CAsyncServiceHandler.Wait(System.Int16)
member.summary: Wait until the request (sRequestId) is processed and its result is returned. If the request id (sRequestId) is zero, it implies the last request. The method returns true if its result is returned. Otherwise, it returns false. Refer to the method CClientSocket.Wait.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.ClientSide.CAsyncServiceHandler.Wait(System.Int16,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait until the request (sRequestId) is processed and its result is returned or it is timed out at the given time (nTimeout) in milliseconds. If the request id (sRequestId) is zero, it implies the last request if available. The method returns true if its result is returned. Otherwise, it returns false. Refer to the method CClientSocket.Wait.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Load(System.String@)
member.summary: Retrieve an integer data first that indicates the coming string length in byte first. Afterwards, retrieve the string (str) from the memory queue according to the integer data. Keep eyes on the difference between this method and the method Pop for string.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Save(System.String)
member.summary: Write an integer data first that indicates the string length in byte. Afterwards, write the string (strData) into the memory queue. Note if the string data is null, the integer data will be -1 or 0xFFFFFFFF. Keep eyes on the difference between this method and the method Push for string.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(System.String@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieve a specified length of string from the memory queue in byte. The method returns the actual number of bytes retrieved. Pay attention to the difference between this method and the method Load.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(System.String@)
member.summary: Retrieve a string from the memory queue. The method returns the number of bytes retrieved.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop``1(``0@)
member.summary: Retrieve a .NET object from the memory queue through the interface IUSerializer. Note that retrieving an object is much faster through IUSerializer than .NET serializer.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Push(SocketProAdapter.IUSerializer)
member.summary: Write a .NET object into the memory queue through the interface IUSerializer. Note that packing an object is much faster through IUSerializer than .NET serializer.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Push(System.Object,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Write a .NET object into the memory queue. If bToNative is set to true and obData contains a DateTime, the DateTime will be converted into VARIANT DATE. If both bToNative and bDecToCY are true, obData will be converted into VARIANT Currency if obData contains a Decimal data. Note that .NET object may be compatible with many native VARIANTs.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Push(System.Object,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Write a .NET object into the memory queue. If bToNative is set to true and obData contains a DateTime, the DateTime will be converted into VARIANT DATE. Note that .NET object may be compatible with many native VARIANTs.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Push(System.Object)
member.summary: Write a .NET object into the memory queue with highest compatibility with native VARIANTs. Note that .NET object may be compatible with many native VARIANTs.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(SocketProAdapter.CSocketProServerException@)
member.summary: Retrieve a CSocketProServerException from the memory queue.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(SocketProAdapter.CUQueue@)
member.summary: Retrieve a CUQueue from main memory queue.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(System.Object@)
member.summary: Retrieve a .NET object from the memory queue. Note that .NET object may be compatible with many native VARIANTs.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Push(System.String)
member.summary: Save a string into the memory queue without saving string length. Keep eyes on difference between this method and the method Save.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(System.Byte[]@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieve a specified size (nLen) of data in byte from the memory queue. The method returns the data size obtained actually in byte.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Pop(System.Byte[]@)
member.summary: Retrieve all data from the memory queue. The method returns the data size obtained actually in byte.

member.name: P:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Size
member.summary: Same as the method GetSize()

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Discard(System.Int32)
member.summary: Discard a given number (nLen) of data. Don't get confused with the method CUQueue::Pop(int %nData)! The method returned the actual number of bytes discarded.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.GetBuffer
member.summary: Get a pointer to packed data in the underlying native memory.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.SetHeadPosition
member.summary: Set head position to 0 and move packed data in buffer as required.

member.summary: Set data size. If buffer is is less than expected size nSize, the underlying buffer is reallocated for a larger buffer size.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.GetSize
member.summary: Returns the packed data size in byte.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.Empty
member.summary: Release all memores back into operation system.

member.name: M:SocketProAdapter.CUQueue.CleanTrack
member.summary: Cleam all memory buffer except the buffer chunk packed with data. Call this method to clean all of secret data left in memory foot print.
